title: nanofeatures: the per-element attribution-cost boundary on Gemma-2-2B and GPT-2
description: Calibrates a cheap gradient-free feature score against gradient attribution patching on SAE features for Gemma-2-2B and GPT-2. The two methods tie on single-token tasks and diverge on distributed circuits, locating the boundary where the expensive method earns its cost, with paired-bootstrap confidence intervals and pre-registered predictions including negative results. The second part of the nano* trilogy on when expensive interpretability is worth its cost.
